seoTitle: Lavender Bay vs Laverstock — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Lavender Bay and Laverstock. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Lavender Bay vs Laverstock: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lavender Bay and Laverstock.
heading: Lavender Bay vs Laverstock — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lavender Bay, New South Wales and Laverstock, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Laverstock has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Lavender Bay (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lavender Bay or Laverstock safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lavender Bay has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Laverstock scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lavender Bay scores 60/100 for bushfire risk. Laverstock scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lavender Bay or Laverstock?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Laverstock has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.38% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
